title: [BUG] containers not connected to the network
body:
When creating a network and containers via this library, containers are not connected to the network. Podman is running in rootless mode, with pasta configured. It all works as expected if I use podman cli directly.

Podman binary 5.4.2
podman  python lib    5.6.0

<details>
<summary>Show code</summary>

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3

from podman import PodmanClient


SOCKET = "unix:///tmp/podman.sock"
NETWORK_NAME = "ping-net"
CONTAINER_A = "ping-alpha"
CONTAINER_B = "ping-bravo"
IMAGE = "docker.io/library/alpine:latest"


def remove_container_if_exists(client: PodmanClient, name: str) -> None:
    if client.containers.exists(name):
        container = client.containers.get(name)
        container.remove(force=True)


def remove_network_if_exists(client: PodmanClient, name: str) -> None:
    if client.networks.exists(name):
        network = client.networks.get(name)
        network.remove()


def main() -> None:
    with PodmanClient(base_url=SOCKET) as client:
        remove_container_if_exists(client, CONTAINER_A)
        remove_container_if_exists(client, CONTAINER_B)
        remove_network_if_exists(client, NETWORK_NAME)

        client.images.pull(IMAGE)
        network = client.networks.create(NETWORK_NAME, dns_enabled=True)

        container_a = client.containers.create(
            image=IMAGE,
            name=CONTAINER_A,
            hostname=CONTAINER_A,
            command=["sleep", "infinity"],
            detach=True,
            networks={network.name: {}},
        )
        container_b = client.containers.create(
            image=IMAGE,
            name=CONTAINER_B,
            hostname=CONTAINER_B,
            command=["sleep", "infinity"],
            detach=True,
            networks={network.name: {}},
        )

        container_a.start()
        container_b.start()

        exit_code, output = container_a.exec_run(["ping", "-c", "3", CONTAINER_B])
        if exit_code != 0:
            raise RuntimeError(f"Ping failed with exit code {exit_code}: {output}")

        print(output.decode("utf-8", errors="ignore"))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

```
</details> 

<details>
<summary>Output</summary>

```sh
./test.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/workspaces/backend-python/./test.py", line 62, in <module>
    main()
    ~~~~^^
  File "/workspaces/backend-python/./test.py", line 56, in main
    raise RuntimeError(f"Ping failed with exit code {exit_code}: {output}")
RuntimeError: Ping failed with exit code 1: b"\x02\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x1fping: bad address 'ping-bravo'\n"

```
</details> 

<details>
<summary>Podman output</summary>

```sh
seky@bugmaker ~> podman --version
podman version 5.4.2
seky@bugmaker ~> podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND         CREATED        STATUS        PORTS       NAMES
46b40b4d7805  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  sleep infinity  3 minutes ago  Up 3 minutes              ping-alpha
0a22506ed167  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  sleep infinity  3 minutes ago  Up 3 minutes              ping-bravo
seky@bugmaker ~> podman network inspect ping-net

[
     {
          "name": "ping-net",
          "id": "be0c4d2918ae7f2201e44d305702c74752ac8f3134abaa7f0534174aebbda73a",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman1",
          "created": "2026-01-16T09:49:28.282331484+01:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
                    "gateway": "10.89.0.1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": false,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": true,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          },
          "containers": {}
     }
]

```
</details>

comments.body:
I have tracked it down to here:
https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/podman/api/http_utils.py#L96

if you specify network as 
networks={network.name: {}},

then it will be filtered out as emtpy value in this function and never appended to the actual api call

update:
even after adding the exception for networks in _filter_values it was not working, but at least I was getting valid api errors then. 

It seems that both network_mode: bridge and networks needs to be used in combination.

then api request look like this:
```
{"Networks": {"ping-net": {}}, "command": ["sleep", "infinity"], "image": "docker.io/library/alpine:latest", "name": "ping-bravo", "netns": {"nsmode": "bridge"}}
```

and it is working as expected
ty, I have no comments to the issue since you opened a PR to address it. a left a couple of comments there
